name: Confirmshaming
summary: Using guilt-inducing language on the opt-out choice to steer the user toward the opt-in.

evidence.claim: Brignull's canonical dark-patterns taxonomy lists 'confirmshaming' (sometimes called 'manipulinks') as a manipulation pattern.
The FTC's 2023 report 'Bringing Dark Patterns to Light' lists emotionally-loaded framing of choice architecture as an example of deceptive design.

body.description: The opt-out button uses emotionally-loaded, self-disparaging, or guilt-inducing language to discourage users from choosing it. Examples: 'No thanks, I don't want to save money', 'I don't care about my family's safety', 'I hate good deals'.

detectionHeuristics.description: Scan the text content of buttons/links; run text-match above on the inner text.

body.llmVerifyPrompt: You are evaluating a modal or button-choice UI for confirmshaming. The opt-in option offers something to the user; the opt-out option is phrased in a way that disparages the user or their choice. For each button or link, classify as 'neutral' or 'confirmshaming' and explain briefly.
body.remediation: Flag the modal or choice UI. Highlight the confirmshaming text. Recommend the user make the choice based on the underlying value proposition, not the emotional framing.
